sections.label: USER PROMPT
sections.content:
The code executed 2 actions in the previous sequence.
Executed actions: RIGHT, SPACE.
You are still on the same level.
Current state: step 40, level 1.
Valid actions right now: UP, DOWN, LEFT, RIGHT, SPACE.
Only tool: `python`. It receives `current_frame`, `previous_frame`, `history`, `transitions`, `last_transition`, `valid_actions`, `last_action_result`, and `action(actions)`. It also provides `last_animation` (per-action frames of your last `action(...)` call) and `frame_stats` (how much this game has animated so far) -- optional; use them only if you judge the intermediate motion informative rather than incidental.
Only letter-coded board views and lightweight metadata are exposed; raw numeric color IDs are not available.
Keep tool output compact: use `current_frame.segmentation` as the primary view, and `current_frame.ascii` only for a small specific region; never print full boards.
For the most recent change, compare `previous_frame` to `current_frame`, or `last_transition.before_frame` to `last_transition.after_frame`; `history[-1].frame` is the current frame, not the previous one.
Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.
Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.
Below you are provided with the current world model from the previous turn. The default behavior is to copy it and add or remove things based on the evidence that you gathered. BEFORE EXECUTING NEW ACTIONS YOU MUST ALWAYS GIVE THE REVISED VERSION OF THE WORLD MODEL.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it, but stop immediately if a result reports `game_over`, `level_completed`, or `done`.
Working world model carried from earlier turns:
- World model: SPACE activated the created box at [32-35, 36-39] → turned white at [32-35, 35-39]! Player was at same row level (32-35), to the LEFT of the box. Activation rule: player at same rows as box, adjacent to it, then SPACE turns box white. Remaining boxes: left [28-31, 16-19] and bottom-center [36-39, 32-35].
- Plan: Find player position, then navigate to activate remaining boxes.
- Revise any item above immediately if `current_frame` or `history` contradicts it.
end of world model. 
Focus on what changed most recently in `history`, update the target environment change if needed, and separate gameplay-object changes from HUD-only changes.
When ready, call `action(actions)` from inside the `python` tool with the best valid action or ordered batch selected by your code. If your code has found a reliable short sequence, prefer batching it in one call.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it.
If you include assistant text before a tool call, keep it short and use it to update the world model. Helpful optional prefixes are `World model:`, `Goal model:`, `Action model:`, `Recent findings:`, `Open questions:`, `Plan:`, and `Cross-level notes:`.
When calling `python`, emit exactly the tool-call format shown elsewhere in this prompt for this model. Use only that format; do not add markdown fences, prose wrappers, or alternate tool-call syntax. Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.
EFFICIENCY BUDGET — your score on each level is (baseline_actions / your_actions)^2, so every wasted action costs you quadratically.
Level 1: you have used 39 of about 71 target actions so far.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Now I can see the full picture:
- Player at [32-35, 32-34] (4x3, green N)
- Activated box at [32-35, 35-39] (white W, 4x5)
- Left box at [28-31, 16-19] (4x4, charcoal c)
- Bottom-center box at [36-39, 32-35] (4x4, charcoal c)
- Horizontal bar at [28-31, 28-39] (blue b with gray g inside)
The player is at rows 32-35, directly above the bottom-center box at rows 36-39. The player's columns are 32-34, and the box's columns are 32-35. The player is almost aligned with the box (player cols 32-34 vs box cols 32-35).
To activate the bottom-center box, I need to be at the same row level as the box (rows 36-39). I can move DOWN to get there. But moving DOWN would keep the player at 4x3 shape (since I last moved RIGHT).
Wait, let me reconsider the activation rule. The first activation:
- Player at [29-31, 44-47] (3x4), box at [24-27, 44-47] (4x4)
- Player was BELOW the box, same columns
The second activation:
- Player at [32-35, 32-34] (4x3), box at [32-35, 36-39] (4x4)
- Player was to the LEFT of the box, same rows
So the activation rule seems to be: player needs to be at the same row range OR column range as the box, and adjacent to it.
For the bottom-center box at [36-39, 32-35]:
- I could be at rows 36-39, cols 28-31 (to the LEFT)
- Or at rows 36-39, cols 36-39 (to the RIGHT)
- Or at rows 40-43, cols 32-35 (BELOW)
Currently the player is at [32-35, 32-34], which is ABOVE the box. The player's bottom edge (row 35) is adjacent to the box's top edge (row 36). The player's columns (32-34) overlap with the box's columns (32-35) but don't fully match (player is 3 cols wide, box is 4 cols wide).
Let me try moving DOWN to get to rows 36-39, then try to position to the LEFT or RIGHT of the box.
Actually, wait. Let me try SPACE from the current position first. The player is at [32-35, 32-34] and the box is at [36-39, 32-35]. The player is directly above the box, with overlapping columns. Maybe SPACE works from above too?
Actually, I should try SPACE first since the player is adjacent to the box (row 35 touches row 36).

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content:
World model: Player at [32-35, 32-34] (4x3), directly above bottom-center box at [36-39, 32-35]. Player's bottom edge (row 35) touches box's top edge (row 36). Columns partially overlap (32-34 vs 32-35). Left box at [28-31, 16-19] still needs activation.
Plan: Try SPACE from current position (above the box) to see if it activates. If not, move to the side.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Try SPACE from current position (above the box)
result = action(['SPACE'])
print(f"SPACE result: board_changed={last_action_result['board_changed']}, gameplay_changed={last_action_result['gameplay_changed']}")

seg = current_frame.segmentation
print("\nBoxes after SPACE:")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] in ['c', 'G', 'W', 'N'] and n['pixels'] >= 8:
        b = n['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        print(f"  color={n['color']}, rows [{min(rows)}-{max(rows)}], cols [{min(cols)}-{max(cols)}], px={n['pixels']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: TOOL RESULT: python
sections.content:
SPACE result: board_changed=True, gameplay_changed=True

Boxes after SPACE:
  color=c, rows [28-31], cols [16-19], px=12
  color=N, rows [32-35], cols [32-34], px=12
  color=G, rows [32-35], cols [36-39], px=12
  color=c, rows [36-39], cols [32-35], px=12
  color=c, rows [63-63], cols [51-63], px=13
